proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Proximal Femur Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite proximal femur model linking head, neck, trochanters, calcar, and hip joint.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
Extracapsular metaphyseal zone between neck and subtrochanteric diaphysis.
Medial proximal femur attachment for iliopsoas; key subtrochanteric landmark.
Lateral proximal femur with abductor insertions; intertrochanteric fixation landmark.
Dense medial femoral neck cortical buttress supporting head-neck load transfer.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Medial Femoral Circumflex Artery

metadata.description: Dominant retinacular blood supply to the femoral head; vulnerable in neck fractures.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.
Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.
Intracapsular proximal femur fracture with head viability and AVN risk concerns.
Displacement-based femoral neck fracture classification framework.
Partial hip replacement for select displaced femoral neck fractures in older adults.
Femoral head osteonecrosis after compromised retinacular blood supply.
Parallel screw fixation for selected femoral neck fracture patterns.
Open reduction internal fixation of femoral neck fracture.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Displaced Femoral Neck Fracture

metadata.description: Radiographic displacement pattern altering fixation versus arthroplasty calculus.
Failure of femoral neck fracture healing with mechanical symptoms.

relationship_metadata.note: age and function dependent — attending review required

metadata.claim_text: Femoral neck fractures are intracapsular injuries where fracture location and displacement drive fixation versus arthroplasty decisions.
Medial femoral circumflex and retinacular blood supply vulnerability explains AVN risk after femoral neck fracture.
Displaced femoral neck fractures in older adults are often arthroplasty problems rather than screw fixation problems.
Applying geriatric arthroplasty defaults to young active patients with femoral neck fractures without head-preservation discussion.
Garden displacement language helps communicate femoral neck fracture stability and treatment tendency.
State fracture location, displacement, medical optimization urgency, DVT prophylaxis, and likely fixation versus arthroplasty pathway.
Femoral neck nonunion remains a major failure mode when reduction, fixation, or biology are unfavorable.

metadata.trigger: Displaced femoral neck fracture in older adult with low functional reserve or poor healing potential
metadata.action: Discuss hemiarthroplasty versus total hip arthroplasty with attending; document medical optimization and DVT prophylaxis

metadata.trigger: Younger patient with displaced or non-displaced femoral neck fracture where head preservation is prioritized
metadata.action: Urgent anatomic reduction and stable fixation (typically cannulated screws) with AVN surveillance plan
